title: The Problem Solvers | Michael Truell at Cursor
summary: Cursor is an AI-native coding platform that helps engineers and builders ship quality code through agents. Founded by Michael Truell, it has grown from 15 people to 700 and serves over 60% of Fortune 500 companies while collaborating closely with Anthropic.
writeup: A short promotional interview with Cursor's leadership covering the product's founding philosophy, its positioning as an AI coding tool for both professional engineers and non-engineers, and the company's growth trajectory. The through-line is that Cursor's rise is inseparable from the rapid improvement in underlying AI model capabilities, particularly through a tight product-model alignment with Anthropic. The numbers are concrete: from 15 people two years ago to 700, serving over 60% of the Fortune 500.
key_points: Cursor defines its core mission as making AI-assisted software development approachable for professionals and for people with no engineering background, aiming to be the best environment for working with coding agents.
Cursor's leadership cites Anthropic's founding commitment to principles and to being a stable platform for lasting businesses as the primary reason for choosing Anthropic as a deep partner.
The company grew from 15 people to 700 in roughly two years and now serves over 60% of the Fortune 500, placing its software revenue in range with major public software companies.

Cursor's leadership attributes the company's existence in part to the broader revolution in AI model capabilities, describing a productive alignment between product development and model improvements from Anthropic.
